header: ML-DSA-87 per FIPS 204 with KeyGen / Sign / Verify test groups. Wycheproof only covers ML-DSA-87; Tadashi covers all three parameter sets.
Sign tests use rnd to control determinism: rnd = 0^32 produces deterministic FIPS 204 signatures. Tests with non-zero rnd record one specific hedged output.

DeterministicSign.description: Sign with rnd = 0^32 produces a deterministic signature per FIPS 204 hedged mode with zero entropy.

HedgedSign.description: Sign with non-zero rnd produces a hedged (non-deterministic) signature.

Verify.description: Verify(pk, msg, sig, ctx) returns true iff sig was produced by the corresponding sk over the same (msg, ctx).

RoundTrip.description: Verify of a freshly-signed sig succeeds.

LongMessage.description: Message spans multiple SHAKE-256 absorption blocks.

ContextString.description: FIPS 204 §5.2 binds ctx into Mu via the prefix [0x00, len(ctx), ctx].

MaxContext.description: Context length exactly 255 bytes (the FIPS 204 maximum).

ContextTooLong.description: Context length > 255 bytes; FIPS 204 requires rejection.

InputValidation.description: Input fails a structural check that the spec mandates.

MessageBitFlip.description: Message bit modified; sig must not verify.

CTildeBitFlip.description: c~ (challenge seed prefix of sig) bit modified.

ZBitFlip.description: Bit inside the z portion of the signature modified.

HintBitFlip.description: Bit inside the hint section of the signature modified.

HintEncoding.description: Hint encoding pathology: malformed cumulative counts, unsorted indices, or count exceeding omega.

HintCountOverBudget.description: Hint cumulative count exceeds omega; FIPS 204 §7.3 DecodeHintBits must reject.

CTildeEndBitFlip.description: Bit at the end of c~ flipped; catches comparators that short-circuit before reaching the tail.

PKBitFlip.description: Public key bit modified; sig must not verify.

BitFlip.description: Single bit modified somewhere in the (pk, msg, sig, ctx) tuple.

WrongContext.description: Sig was bound to a different context string than the verifier supplies.

CrossKey.description: Sig was produced under a different secret key.

HexByteOrder.description: Bytes are asymmetric; reversed byte order would produce a different result.

HexNibbleOrder.description: Each byte has distinct high and low nibbles.

Pseudorandom.description: Standard correctness check on output value.

tests.comment: ML-DSA-87 KeyGen with all-zero seed. Tests the full KeyGen chain on a degenerate input.
ML-DSA-87 KeyGen with incremental seed (bytes 0..31).
ML-DSA-87 KeyGen with all-0xff seed.
ML-DSA-87 KeyGen with asymmetric seed bytes; nibbles within each byte differ.
Deterministic sign (rnd=0) of empty message with empty context. Empty msg + empty ctx is the simplest signing input.
Deterministic sign of 'abc' with empty context. Standard short-message KAT.
Deterministic sign of 1024-byte message. Exercises the long-message hash path inside Mu derivation.
Deterministic sign with context 'Tadashi' (7 bytes). FIPS 204 §5.2 binds the context into Mu via the domain separator [0, len(ctx), ctx] prefix.
Deterministic sign with max-length context (255 bytes). FIPS 204 limits ctx length to 255 bytes; this tests the boundary.
Hedged sign with non-zero rnd. Same (seed, msg) as the deterministic test but with different rnd; the resulting sig must differ from the deterministic one yet still verify.
Round-trip: sig from 'abc' deterministically signed with the incremental SK, verified against the matching PK.
Message bit 0 flipped (msg becomes 0x60, 'b', 'c'). The Mu hash differs so the challenge differs and verification fails.
Signature bit 0 flipped (low bit of byte 0 of c~). The challenge hash mismatch causes the verification w'_1 reconstruction to differ from the encoded value; reject.
Signature bit at offset 32*8 + 100 flipped (inside z polynomial). Z fails the infinity-norm check OR the verification equation, reject either way.
Public key bit 0 flipped (low bit of rho). A wrong rho expands a different matrix A, so the reconstructed w_1 differs and verify fails.
Sig was bound to empty ctx; verify with ctx='Tadashi'. Domain separation rejects.
Sig produced under SEED_ZERO secret key, verified against SEED_INCR public key. Cross-key forgery.
Last bit of signature flipped (in the hint cumulative-counts area). DecodeHintBits should reject due to bound violation, or verification fails on changed hint set.
Last byte of signature set to 0xFF, which represents a cumulative hint count of 255, far exceeding omega (= 80 for ML-DSA-87, 55 for 65, 75 for 87). DecodeHintBits must reject.
Last bit of c~ (challenge seed prefix, byte 63 bit 7 for ML-DSA-87) flipped. Catches comparators that short-circuit before reaching the end of c~.
Verify with ctx length 256 (over the 255-byte FIPS 204 limit). Verify must return false without attempting the cryptographic check.
